entities.name: OSCILLATING TROUGH-TYPE FERTILIZER APPLICATOR
entities.description: A mechanical device designed to dispense fertilizer using an oscillating trough to ensure controlled metering and distribution.

entities.name: METERING
entities.description: The process of measuring and controlling the amount of fertilizer released by the applicator.

entities.name: DISTRIBUTION PATTERN
entities.description: The spatial arrangement and uniformity of fertilizer as it is applied to the ground.

entities.name: FERTILIZER
entities.description: The nutrient-rich material applied to soil to enhance plant growth.

entities.name: RAKER STRIP
entities.description: A key component of the oscillating trough-type mechanism that influences the quantity and distribution of fertilizer.

entities.name: TROUGH
entities.description: A key component of the oscillating trough-type mechanism that holds and releases fertilizer.

entities.name: ANGLE OF OSCILLATION
entities.description: The angle of the trough's movement relative to the vertical center line of the hopper, affecting distribution and application rate.

entities.name: QUANTITY
entities.description: The amount of fertilizer dispensed, which varies based on the centrality of the raker strip and trough.

entities.name: RATE OF OSCILLATION
entities.description: The frequency of the trough's movement, which affects the uniformity of fertilizer distribution.

entities.name: RATE OF APPLICATION
entities.description: The amount of fertilizer applied per unit area, which can be adjusted by changing the angle of oscillation.
